text.title: tool-migration-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for tool-migration-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Decommissioning the source tool immediately after cutover success.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "Inevitably someone needs to look up what was decided in PROJ-456" — this is guaranteed to happen within days. Premature decommission forces re-provisioning under pressure.

failure-mode.description: Agents running migration without a resumable checkpoint file per issue ID.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Rate limits and network failures crash mid-batch migrations. Without a checkpoint file (last successfully migrated ID), the entire run must restart from the beginning.

failure-mode.description: Agents migrating @mentions without user ID resolution.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Source and target user IDs differ. Unresolved @mentions silently become plain text or broken links. Resolve user ID mapping in advance; log and drop unresolvable mentions.

failure-mode.description: Accepting an LLM-estimated migration cost without a 2x multiplier.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM migration estimates are systematically low; they omit training, parallel-run period, and integration rebuild cost. Multiply by 2x minimum and add a parallel-run period.
